name: Geometry
domain: Euclidean and non-Euclidean theorems, curvature, symmetry groups, topology
core_insight: Space has intrinsic properties measurable without embedding. Curvature determines whether parallel lines meet. The Euler characteristic is a topological invariant that survives any smooth deformation — the deepest fingerprint of a shape.

key_equations.name: Gaussian Curvature
key_equations.why: Product of principal curvatures: intrinsic — survives bending without stretching

key_equations.name: Gauss-Bonnet Theorem
key_equations.why: Total curvature of a surface equals 2π times its Euler characteristic — topology controls geometry

key_equations.name: Pythagorean Theorem
key_equations.why: Foundational: right triangles, distance, and the metric structure of flat space

seed_prompt: ☆ What is the Euler characteristic? Topology answers before geometry. Curvature lives in the surface itself.
